# Figure description: Страт_колонки_скважин_черт_49_л_8.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/drill_holes_data/Страт_колонки_скважин_черт_49_л_8.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **composite stratigraphic/lithological borehole log (геологический разрез)** — specifically a multi-well correlation panel showing geological sections (разрезы) from three boreholes plotted side-by-side to allow stratigraphic correlation. This is a standard Soviet-era lithological column format showing rock types, formation boundaries, and stratigraphic unit designations at depth.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure presents **geological sections from three boreholes**, labeled as follows (left to right):

1. **Геологический разрез по скважине П-224** — Borehole П-224, map sheet **Л-42-XXXIII** (left column)
2. **Геологический разрез по скважине П-237** — Borehole П-237, map sheet **Л-42-XXXIV** (central/tallest column)
3. **Геологический разрез по скважине П-236** — Borehole П-236, map sheet **Л-42-VXXIV** (right column)

The boreholes appear to penetrate Carboniferous and Devonian stratigraphic sequences in northern Kazakhstan, with total depths appearing to reach into **Devonian** (D) basement. Depth ranges extend to several hundred metres (individual units annotated in metres).

---

## Key Content

The figure shows **three vertical lithological columns** drawn at a consistent vertical scale (масштаб 1:1000 per the title block), placed side by side for stratigraphic correlation. Horizontal tie lines connect equivalent stratigraphic horizons between columns, indicating correlation of formation boundaries across the three borehole locations.

**Lithological patterns visible** (using standard Soviet geological symbols):
- **Dotted/stippled pattern**: sandstones or coarse clastic rocks
- **Wavy/curved line pattern**: likely clays, mudstones, or argillites
- **Cross (+) pattern**: possibly igneous intrusions or crystalline basement
- **Brick/rectangular pattern**: limestones or dense carbonates
- **"v v v" pattern** (chevrons): volcanic rocks or tuffs (visible at base of П-237 and П-236)
- **"г г г" pattern**: granitoids or crystalline basement (visible at base of П-224 and П-237)

**Stratigraphic units labeled** (using Cyrillic stratigraphic notation — Carboniferous subdivisions С₁, С₂, С₃ and Devonian D):

*Borehole П-237 (central, tallest — deepest penetration):*
- С₁с₁² — ~47m (87m) [near top]
- С₁с₁¹ — ~165m (225?) [mid-upper section]
- N₁с₁² — ~200m
- С₂с₁² — ~530m
- С₃с₁² — ~20m
- С₃+₄с — ~50m
- С₄+₅?? — ~35m [illegible partial]
- В₁? — ~20m
- D₁₋₂ [at base — Devonian]

*Borehole П-224 (left):*
- С₁с₁² — ~30m
- N₁с₁² — ~200m
- С₃с₁² — ~20m
- С₄+₅Ar — ~50m (possibly Artinskian?)
- С₁Ar — ~30m [illegible]
- С₁с — ~40m [near base]
- D₁₋₂ [at base]

*Borehole П-236 (right):*
- С₁с₁² — ~75m [top]
- С₁с₁² — ~130m [middle]
- С₃с₁² — ~80m
- С₄Ar — ~30m [illegible]
- С₁с — ~20m
- С₁с — ~20m
- D₁₋₂ [at base, shown with chevron/volcanic symbol]

The **correlation lines** connecting the columns show that major stratigraphic boundaries (particularly Carboniferous subdivision boundaries and the Devonian unconformity/basement contact) are traceable across all three wells, though with varying thicknesses — indicating lateral facies changes or structural relief on the formation tops.

The **Devonian basement** (D₁₋₂) is reached in all three boreholes but at different depths, suggesting the pre-Carboniferous surface has some topographic/structural relief.
